mappings.rationaleAI: Ecology spanning population dynamics, metapopulations, and migration (A), interspecific interactions including food webs and trophic levels (B), and community structure including niches and species diversity (C).
Extinction as a factor in fluctuations of stable populations (A.4) and as a negative interspecific interaction through competition, predation, and disease (B.2.b).
Food as a medium for heterotrophic nutrition and predation interactions within community structure (C.1.c).
Community function through energy flow (C.2) and community classification using the biome or formation as units (C.5.b).
Human migration as a form of population movement including emigration, dispersion, and the influence of barriers (A.5).
Natural selection acting on interspecific interactions with ecological, evolutionary, and biogeographical aspects on the population level (B.3).
Urbanization as a demographic shift affecting human population structure (A.7).
Fish as the largest vertebrate assemblage contributing to population characteristics including age and sex distribution (A.2) and interspecific interactions in aquatic food webs (B.2).
Flower-pollinator coevolution as a positive interspecific interaction of mutualism (B.2.c).
Sex distribution within biological populations as a measurable characteristic (A.2.a).
Seasonal variation driving periodicity and population changes in community time relations (C.1.b).
Exponential growth models relevant to growth of populations including growth form and carrying capacity (A.3).
Factors affecting the structure of human populations including demography, urbanization, and environmental impact (A.7).
Evolutionary change through interactions between populations of different species at the population level (B.3), including coevolution, cooperation, and extinction.
Animal populations exhibiting intraspecific interactions (B.1) and interspecific interactions in food webs and ecological communities (B.2).
Forest layered structure illustrating vertical stratification patterns in community structure (C.1.a) and forest succession as community growth toward stable condition (C.3).
Altitudinal climate and ecosystem zonation on mountains illustrating landscape patterns and environmental gradients (C.4.a).
Ageing affecting mortality rates and age distribution within biological populations (A.2.b).
Genetic differences and their distribution within populations as measurable population characteristics (A.2.a).
Hereditary transmission governing genetic differences and their distribution within populations (A.2.a).
Biological taxonomy providing the classification framework for community classification bases (C.5).
Primate social structures as intraspecific interactions (B.1) and primate niche occupation and species diversity (C.1.d).
Viruses as agents of negative interspecific interactions through parasitic pathogenesis affecting population dynamics (B.2.b).
